observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor observe due diligence in the exercise of his basic powers, duties and functions by directing the Municipal Accountant and other concern offices to cause timely submission of the financial statements and reports, disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents in accordance with the requirements of the law and COA Circulars.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) direct the Municipal Accounting Office in the observance of Section 111 and 114 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 on the accomplishment of the GLs and SLs in the prescribed format, in aid of audit; and (b) consider the implementation of the Electronic New Government Accounting System (e-NGAS) to enhance internal controls, improve the timeliness and reliability of financial reporting, and support compliance with government accounting rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. through the Municipal Accountant, strengthen the internal control system over disbursement processing and documentation to ensure that (i) DVs are properly supported with corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers and that the completeness of required supporting documents is thoroughly reviewed prior to signing and approving Box A of the DV and (ii) ensure that the DVs are faithfully and accurately accomplished by consistently assigning DV numbers, indicating the DV date, and recording the date of approval; and
b. direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure the faithful completion of the DVs by certifying the "Funds Available" portion and indicating the corresponding date of approval.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Treasurer (i) account for all unclaimed checks which had been outstanding for over six months from date of issue wherein the original DVs and supporting documents are still in the custody of the treasury office and cancel these stale checks; (ii) require all Accountable Officers to maintain and regularly update their respective cashbooks in the prescribed format provided by the LTOM;
b. Municipal Accountant to update the SLs and observe strict adherence to Sections 9 and 10 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II, to support the balances in the general ledger; and
c. Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records as at December 31, 2024 and take-up the necessary adjusting entries in their respective records and submit a reconciliation statement thereon.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor adopt the perpetual inventory method per Section 114 of the MNGAS and:
a. direct the Property Custodian to (i) maintain and record the receipts and issuances of the supplies and/or inventories in the corresponding Supplies and Stock Cards; and (ii) consolidate RIS for which supplies and materials were issued and prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and forward to Accounting Unit; and
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain SLC for each inventory account.

We reiterated our recommendations that the:
a. Municipal Accountant investigate and exert extra effort to determine the details of unaccounted/unidentified PPE items, and if found futile, avail of the sunset provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for one-time cleansing of PPE accounts;
b. Municipal GSO and the Municipal Accountant to retrieve relevant records to maintain and update the required SLCs, RPLC and PPELC, respectively, per category of PPE and henceforth maintain the same in accordance with Sections 13, 15 and 120 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
c. Inventory Committee, Municipal GSO and Municipal Accountant, after completing the actual physical count of PPE and updating the required ledger cards, perform reconciliation of the physical count and ledger entries, to establish correctness and validity of the account balances presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Accountant:
a. thoroughly review the disbursement vouchers and ensure that all necessary and relevant documents are supported before certifying the claims for payment of land acquisition; and
b. in coordination with the MPDC, secure and submit the necessary documents supporting the purchase of lot in accordance with Item 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to enable the Audit Team to conduct proper review and evaluation of the disbursements to determine its validity, legality and propriety.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Human Resource Management Officer to (i) formulate and issue approved policy guidelines on the hiring of COS/JO personnel promulgating the standards for uniform application; (ii) require the execution of contracts of service prior to the hiring of COS/JO personnel to legally bind the parties, observing the provisions of CSC Resolution No. 020790 dated June 5, 2002; (iii) provide the standard daily wage rate to COS/JO workers as approved by the local Sanggunian; and (iv) comply with the mandated daily minimum wage at rates prescribed by the Regional Tripartite Wages and Productivity Board; and the
b. Municipal Accountant ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012 – 001 dated June 14, 2012 on the submission of required supporting documents before processing the payment of wages of COS/JO personnel.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman to:
a. facilitate the prompt submission of the lacking documents and/or correction of deficiencies to COA in order to prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions; and
b. strictly comply with the documentary requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the conduct of public biddings and processing of payments of infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor observe due diligence in the exercise of his basic powers, duties and functions by directing the Municipal Accountant to (a) collectively cause timely submission of the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents and thoroughly inspect these documents prior to transportation to the city to prevent any unwarranted delays; and (b) enforce the timely submission of the financial reports and supporting schedules within the period prescribed and faithfully comply with the requirements of the law and COA Circulars.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor observe due diligence in the exercise of his basic powers, duties and functions by directing the Municipal Accountant and other concern offices to cause timely submission of the financial statements and reports, disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents in accordance with the requirements of the law and COA Circulars.
observations.reason: Observation No. 1 of this AAR. The CY 2024 FS and financial transaction documents were still not submitted to the Audit Team on the time. The Municipal Accountant made efforts to augment the accounting personnel while evaluating the manpower requirements of the Municipal Accounting Office if there is a need to create plantilla positions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to (a) maintain and regularly update the cashbooks and submit duly certified true copies to the Audit Team for review and validation of cash balances; and the Municipal Accountant to (b) take extra efforts in updating the SLs and observe strict adherence to Sections 9 and 10 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II, to support the balances of the controlling accounts in the general ledger; and (c) ensure submission of bank statements for all accounts as attached to the BRS pursuant to Item 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-11.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Treasurer to maintain and regularly update cashbooks and submit the duly certified true copies to the Audit Team for review and validation of cash balances; Municipal Accountant to take extra efforts in updating the SLs and observe strict adherence to Sections 9 and 10 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II, to support the balances of the controlling accounts in the general ledger; ensure submission of bank statements for all accounts as attached to the BRS pursuant to Item 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-11.
observations.reason: The CY 2024 FS and financial transaction documents were still not submitted to the Audit Team on the time. The Municipal Treasurer committed to submit certified copies of cashbooks and the Municipal Accountant to update SLs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU: a. strictly observe the general principles that all payments should be made directly to payee/supplier through the issuance of check except those expressly allowed under COA Circular 97-002, and when payment by check is impractical or impossible to do so; b. implement the use of modes of procurement provided under the Revised IRR of RA 9184; c. refrain from granting additional cash advance if the previous ones remained unliquidated or unsettled; and d. stop the practice of transferring cash advances to another accountable officer.
observations.managementAction: Said cash withdrawals were neither settled nor returned. The Municipal Treasurer and Accounting Office will stop the practice and will implement stricter controls. The Municipal Mayor directed the Municipal Treasurer to observe proper disbursement processes.
observations.reason: Payments to suppliers, creditors and employees were implemented to be made by check; services of some SDOs were terminated and efforts to centralize disbursement processing undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. stop granting cash advances to SDOs in excess of the maximum cash accountability covered by his/her fidelity bond; and b. monitor and ensure compliance with the bonding requirement among its SDOs.
observations.managementAction: Stop granting cash advances to SDOs in excess of bonded amounts and monitor compliance with bonding requirements. Copies of fidelity bond certificates to be secured.
observations.reason: Copies of fidelity bond certificates were not submitted for verification but the LGU reported compliance and monitoring measures have been undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor expedite the submission of the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts in line with COA Circular No. 2016-005, establish and monitor credit risk policy, take appropriate actions on unsettled accounts, regularly monitor and analyze receivable accounts to ensure collection, send demand letters to concerned accountable officers and employees with unsettled accounts, and set up allowance for impairment representing estimated anticipated losses.
observations.managementAction: Efforts have been undertaken to reconcile the accounts and break down certain account balances. Demand letters were issued to accountable officers and employees. A request for write-off will be initiated upon completion of reconciliation.
observations.reason: Efforts to reconcile receivable balances are ongoing; no formal write-off completed pending reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct complete physical count of PPE and submit the Report on Physical Count of the Property, Plant and Equipment adhering to prescribed dates; Municipal Accountant investigate and exert extra effort to determine the details of unidentified/for recon PPE items and avail of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for one-time cleansing if necessary; Municipal GSO and Municipal Accountant retrieve relevant records to maintain and update the required SLCs, RPLC and PPELC; and Inventory Committee, Municipal GSO and Municipal Accountant, after completing physical count and updating ledger cards, perform reconciliation of physical count and ledger entries.
observations.managementAction: The inventory of PPE, particularly those related to real property and road networks, could not be fully completed due to remote location of certain assets and lack of source documents and archived records. Management affirmed commitment to implement recommendations and the Municipal Accountant worked to reduce unaccounted items.
observations.reason: One-time cleansing is still ongoing; some PPE remain unverified due to geographical constraints and missing records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, immediately submit to the Audit Team the remaining disbursement vouchers for CY 2023 for post-audit purposes; and henceforth, strictly observe the regular and timely submission of transaction documents in accordance with Item 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The remaining DVs were submitted and the Municipal Accountant committed to timely submission henceforth. Copies of vouchers were provided for review.
observations.reason: The DVs and supporting documents for the period were submitted to the Audit Team upon request; improvements in submission processes were indicated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Secretariat to submit copies of the perfected government contracts and its supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Copies of contracts and POs were submitted to COA as required within the specified timeframe for subsequent review.
observations.reason: Contracts and POs for the period were forwarded to the Audit Team upon receipt and processing as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC, thru the BAC Secretariat: (a) post in the PhilGEPS website the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed for all procurements done thru public bidding and all instances of alternative methods of procurement, except for contracts with approved budget of ₱50,000.00 and below; and (b) strictly abide by the posting requirements in the PhilGEPS in compliance with the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed were posted in the PhilGEPS website for procurements done in CY 2023 except those below the threshold; BAC committed to adhere to posting requirements henceforth.
observations.reason: NOAs and NTPs pertaining to CY 2023 were posted in PhilGEPS; some postings were updated late but rectified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the completeness of supporting documents before preparation of vouchers for all future transactions to ensure validity of claims and strictly comply with the documentary requirements as enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the payment of terminal leave benefits.
observations.managementAction: Completeness of terminal leave claims were reviewed before payment and the Municipal Accountant implemented checks to ensure required documents are attached.
observations.reason: Terminal leave benefits were reviewed and validated prior to payment; procedures implemented for future transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource Management Officer to: (a) implement the use of biometric scanner/bundy clock as the official method for recording daily attendance of all personnel of the municipality; (b) compute salaries and wages based on the DTR generated by the biometric scanner; (c) prepare monthly report of undertimes and absences to ensure compliance with CSC policies; and (d) update leave cards accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Bundy clock/biometric scanner installed and leave cards updated; monthly undertime and absence reports to be prepared based on the biometric system.
observations.reason: Bundy clock installed and DTRs used for payroll computation; copies of MRUA were not attached to some DVs but compliance has been improved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) prioritize developing and implementing a strategic human resources plan focused on recruiting and retaining personnel to fill vacant plantilla positions; and (b) conduct regular assessments of staffing needs and establish mechanisms to address gaps and optimize human resources utilization.
observations.managementAction: One plantilla position has been filled and five departments remain under OIC capacity; the Sangguniang Bayan was referred for possible amendment of resolution and staffing assessments undertaken.
observations.reason: Only one plantilla position filled; five remain under supervision of OICs; ongoing efforts to fill vacancies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSWDO submit the SB approved Sangguniang Bayan Resolution prescribing the policies and guidelines on the granting of donations/financial assistance containing, among others, the kind of financial assistance to be given, criteria to qualify for the donation/assistance, limitations in the amounts of grant for each type of financial assistance, documentary requirements for each type of assistance, and well defined roles and responsibilities of the officials processing requests for donations; Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review disbursements to determine completeness and compliance; and Special Disbursing Officer to refrain from paying any DV not bearing required certification and approval of proper officials.
observations.managementAction: SB approved policies and guidelines on donations/financial assistance were submitted; Municipal Accountant to review disbursements and SDO to refrain from unauthorized payments.
observations.reason: Sangguniang Bayan resolution prescribing policies was approved and submitted; some documentary requirements for donations remained incomplete but process improvements instituted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the MPDC, include in the Annual Investment Plan those projects which can readily be implemented during the year and prioritize those projects to ensure optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund; MPDC to coordinate with Municipal Engineer to conduct feasibility or preliminary engineering study prior to appropriation of funds; Municipal Engineer to expedite and strictly monitor implementation of ongoing priority projects while evaluating performance of contractors and compute and hold erring contractors liable for liquidated damages when necessary.
observations.managementAction: Majority of projects funded under the 20% Development Fund for Calendar Year 2024 have been implemented; MPDC coordinated and prepared plans; Municipal Engineer monitoring ongoing projects and addressing contractor performance where needed.
observations.reason: Some projects funded under prior years remain ongoing (e.g., CY 2016 project) with partial completion; implementation of priority projects improved in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRM Officer comply with the regular preparation and timely submission of the LDRRMFIP and monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF with proof of receipt by the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) and the DILG-LGOO; utilize the fund strictly for disaster risk management activities pursuant to RA 10121 and pertinent rules and regulations; review and update annually the LDRRMFIP and incorporate detailed PPAs to be funded from prior years' unutilized QRF and DRRMF-MOOE transferred to the special trust fund; Municipal Accountant ensure supporting documents are complete; Municipal Budget Officer maintain a separate RAAO for the LDRRMF and submit same to the Audit Team; and BAC to strictly enforce compliance with RA 9184 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: The DRRM Officer committed to prompt submission of monthly reports via email. Management indicated some expenses were approved by DRRM Council and necessary work to update LDRRMFIP will be undertaken. Municipal Accountant to ensure supporting documents. Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate RAAO.
observations.reason: DRRM Officer to include prior year unutilized balances in updated MDRRMFIP; certain documentation and RAAO establishment remain in progress but monthly utilization reports initiated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) monitor compliance in the formulation and maintenance of the GAD Agenda, Code and Database; (b) observe deadlines in the submission of the GAD AR, properly endorsed by the DILG and complete with essential attachments to facilitate proper evaluation and assessment of the implementation of the GAD PAPs.

observations.reason: Supporting documents for some GAD AR submissions were incomplete or lacking but the GAD documents were prepared and submitted for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to (i) exert extra effort in locating the supporting papers and other reports pertaining to the unremitted BIR withholding taxes amounting to ₱4,151,170.03 for CY 2016 and preceding years; (ii) coordinate with the former OIC-Municipal Accountant to shed some light regarding the matter; (iii) thereafter, cause the immediate remittance of the amount to the BIR and/or record the necessary adjustments in the books of account, as appropriate; and (iv) henceforth, ensure strict compliance with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998 to avoid any repercussion.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting, Budget and Treasury Office already combined all their efforts to locate the related vouchers and other supporting documents in question for subsequent remittance to BIR. Inquiry with Mr. Kiram Abbas, former OIC- Municipal Accountant, yielded no result as he also does not have the record of the transactions composing the balances of Due to BIR account.
The Municipal Accountant already went to the BIR Office with the intention to remit the withholding taxes of calendar year 2019. They were in constant communication with the BIR Tax Agent, who advised them to accomplish the necessary BIR forms and continue with the payment procedures. However, after endorsing the forms to her supervisor, the municipal check payments of the remittances were not accepted. They were informed that they need to settle first their previous withholding taxes and that they needed the required vouchers to ascertain the total amount owed by the Municipality to the BIR.
observations.reason: Difficulties in locating relevant reports and documents for the unremitted BIR withholding taxes.

      draw the necessary journal entries to reflect the correct  balance  of

operating expenses accounts; (ii) review charges to  account  “05-02-99-990”

or  asset  account  and  crediting   account   “05-02-99-990”;   and   (iii)
consistently compare estimates to actual  expenses  in  the  preparation  of
Annual  Budget  based  on   the   accurate   classification   of   financial

        statements.

        responsibilities  in  assisting  the  local   accountant   in   the

        Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the OIC – Municipal
Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team the trial balance, journals
and financial statements of all funds and subsidiary schedules of GL accounts
for the months of January to December 2018; and (b) ensure that the required
financial and other reports and statements are prepared and submitted by the
concerned agency officials in such form and within the period prescribed by the
aforementioned regulations.

22. We recommended that:
1. the OIC - Municipal Treasurer submit within five (5) days after the end of
the month the monthly reports of disbursements together with the
disbursement vouchers and payrolls to the Municipal Accountant to allow its
prompt recording in the books of accounts;
2. the OIC - Municipal Accountant (a) coordinate with the OIC - Municipal
Treasurer to ensure the prompt submission of the disbursement
vouchers/payrolls and related reports; and (b) ensure its timely submission
including the financial statements and other accounting reports to the
Auditor within the first ten days of the ensuing month as required under
Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009;
and
3. the LCE initiate the necessary administrative/criminal action in case of
unjustified failure/refusal to comply with timely rendition of accounts by
subordinate officials.

28. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the immediate
submission of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) for
CY 2017 on the Municipality and henceforth, observe strict compliance to COA
Memorandum No. 2014-002 dated March 18, 2014 and Section 88 of RA No.
10924 on the submission of AAPSI within 60 days from receipt of the AAR.

34. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (i) exert extra effort in finding
qualified applicants and appoint a permanent Municipal Accountant who posses
the qualifications mandated under Article Four, Section 474 of RA No. 7160;
and (ii) explain as to why the Municipality was not able to hire/appoint qualified
accountant despite repeated recommendation regarding the matter.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accounting
Office headed by the OIC–Municipal Accountant to (a) establish the breakdown and
component of every major accounts reflected in the submitted Financial Statement as
of December 31, 2018 and maintain subsidiary ledgers for each account whenever
necessary; (b) reconstruct the GL for CY 2018 to prove the ending balances of the
accounts and submit to the Audit Team the schedule of subsidiary ledger balances of
the controlling accounts in the General Ledger as of December 31, 2018 as supporting
schedule to the financial statements; and (c) review and be guided by the NGAS
Manual for LGUs and PD No. 1445 for the procedures and appropriate accounting
formats to be used in recording the financial transactions of the Municipality.

We recommended that the OIC - Municipal Budget Officer and the OIC–
Municipal Accountant strictly observe Item No. 2 of COA Circular No. 2005-001 and
Section 4, paragraph (h) Volume I of COA Circular No. 2002-003 and henceforth,
maintain the required registries of appropriations/allotments and obligations in
order to avoid juggling of funds, for internal control purposes and facilitate the
preparation of Statement of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation (SAAOB)
and Statement of Comparison Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA).
The management committed that they will abide with the audit recommendations.
For CY 2019, the newly appointed OIC-Municipal Budget Officer started maintaining the
required registries as well as monitoring from the Municipal Accountant’s Office to
facilitate preparation of SAAOB and SCBAA.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the
OIC - Municipal Budget Officer, to prepare and submit the Statement of Comparison
of Budget and Actual Amounts for CY 2018 and ensure that material
variances/differences between budget and actual amounts are properly reviewed,
reconciled and adequately disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the installation and
implement proper internal control system and appoint/designate competent
accounting personnel in order to achieve proper record keeping, assure completeness
of the supporting documents in order to avoid possible loss or misuse of
government funds in accordance with the guidelines, policies and procedures of
Sections 47 to 49 of the GAAM, Volume III and Section 124 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (i) re-assess their program
planning and budget execution processes to ascertain and pinpoint the cause/s of
cash deficit and to work out and assertively pursue feasible solutions that can
address the issue involving the fiscal operations of the Municipality; (ii) ensure that
monies held in trust are used solely for the purpose for which it was
created or received and prioritize settlement of trust obligations; (iii) control
disbursements based on available funds/appropriations and not merely on available
cash balance; and (iii) provide satisfactory justification/explanation for the
deficiencies noted herein.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Planning
and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer (i) demand the PGP to
issue Fund Utilization Report in the amount of ₱632,928.00 and follow up on the status
39
of the entire Inter-LGU Alliance Projects; (ii) demand from the PGP the immediate
return of the unexpended fund transfers; and (iii) provide satisfactory explanation as
to why there was no monitoring and coordination of the implementation of the Inter-
LGU Alliance Projects.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor, through the Inventory Committee, conduct the physical
inventory of all properties of the LGU, prepare and reconcile the RPCPPE
with the accounting records, and furnish the Audit Team with the RPCPPE.
During the actual count, the type, description, serial number, acquisition date,
name/designation of employee accountable, location and condition of the
property counted shall be indicated in the Inventory Sheet. Serially pre-
numbered inventory tags shall also be pasted on the counted item/property for
easy identification; and
b. Municipal GSO and the OIC – Municipal Accountant maintain the required
Property Cards per category of PPE, RPLC and PPELC, respectively, per
category of PPE and henceforth maintain the same in accordance with
Sections 119 and 120 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan
expedite the revision and updating of its Local Revenue Code of 2012 pursuant to
Sections129 and 191 of R.A. No. 7160 to increase the revenue collection of local
taxes to finance more valuable projects and pay promptly the obligations of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the:
a. Local Chief Executive (i) assess the need to engage in consultancy services; (ii)
in case there is really a need for consultants, engage only with experts in their
respective fields that will be selected thru proper procurement process; (iii)
observe rules and regulations on the hiring of consultants; and (iv) ensure that
payment for consultancy services are supported with adequate documentation;
b. Bids and Awards Committee ensure that proper procurement process is always
adhered before engaging consultancy services pursuant to the provisions of RA
No. 9184;
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant submit the complete required documents to support
the payment for consultancy services pursuant to JMC No. 2, series of 1979, and
COA Circular No. 2012-001; and
d. OIC-Municipal Treasurer (i) ensure the proper withholding of taxes due to BIR
from the payment of consultancy services in accordance with Section
2.57.2(a)(6) of Revenue Regulation no. 2-98; and (ii) require authorization
document before releasing payment to person other the payee pursuant to COA
Circular No. 85-248.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal
Treasurer to immediately return/remit to the Bureau of Treasury the amount of
₱1,862,912.51 in full compliance with the Supreme Court ruling on PDAF.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the OIC–
Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the trial balance, journals and
financial statements of all funds, subsidiary schedules of GL accounts and other
financial reports in such form and within the period prescribed by the aforementioned
regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. ensure that the funds corresponding to the 20% Development Fund for CY
2018 and prior years are utilized exclusively for the implementation of the
identified priority development projects embodied in the Annual Investment
Plan (AIP) for CYs 2008-2018;
b. ensure that the Local Development Council, Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer regularly
monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary
interventions may be introduced at the earliest time;
c. submit a satisfactory explanation on the causes/reasons of the delay or non-
implementation of the projects totaling ₱63,099,231.68; and
d. ensure the timely implementation of priority development
programs/projects/activities funded under the 20% DF for the ensuing years
in accordance with the provisions in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal
Development Council, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the
Municipal Engineer to ensure that only programs and projects geared towards the
attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes
that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures are given allocation
under the 20% Development Fund to ensure the full realization of the purpose for
which the fund was created; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC–Municipal
Budget Officer make sure that the use of such fund is restricted only to those priority
development projects identified under the said JMC and refrain from utilizing the
fund for expenditure expressly prohibited under Item 4.0 of the JMC.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the OIC–Municipal Budget Officer and OIC-Municipal
Accountant to revise and resubmit the Notes to the Financial Statement
and include the required information as provided by COA Circular No.
2012-002 and 2014-002 as well as the SAAOB reflecting the status of the
appropriations, allotments, obligations and balances;
b. (i) prioritize the creation of LDRRM Office including its personnel; and
(ii) instruct the Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO) to
hire a permanent MDRRM Officer including three support staff in
compliance with Section 12 of RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 04, 2014; and
c. instruct the MDRRMO-Designate to secure fidelity bond from the
Bureau of Treasury in accordance to Section 4.2 of Treasury Circular
No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the assigned LDRRMO to
furnish the Audit Team with the required Annual LDRRMFIP and the Report on the
Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF for CY 2018, and strictly observe the deadlines
on the submission of these reports as required under Sections 5.1.2, 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of
COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Sections 12 (6) & (7) of the Philippine Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Act of 2010.
Furthermore, ensure that the LDRRMFIP is based on the approved National
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan (NDRRMP) 2011-2028, validated and
accustomed to the local needs encompassing disaster risk reduction and management
such as disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response,
and disaster rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) MDRRMO to
submit a report to MDDRMC on the status of the damage assessment and needs
analysis as well as the completion of disaster recovery and rehabilitation projects
after typhoon Vinta for the Council’s information and guidance in the request for
lifting of declaration of State of Calamity to the SP; and (b) OIC–Municipal
Accountant to comply with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 on the processing of
disbursements of government funds to ensure the validity and propriety of the
transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. MDRRMO to submit (i) the reports and documents that would prove the
legality, propriety and validity of the events and activities conducted by the
MDRRMO; and (ii) a satisfactory explanation why most of the activities were
held at Barangay Mangsee, Balabac;
b. Bids and Awards Committee and BAC Secretariat to (i) explain why the
Abstract of Canvass and the Resolution recommending the use of alternative
mode of procurement was not signed by the BAC Chairman and one of the
BAC Members; (ii) give satisfactory reason as to how and why the suppliers
that have business address located in Puerto Princesa City were chosen to
supply meals and snacks for MDRRM activities in Barangay Mangsee; and (iii)
always adhere to the guidelines and procedures of Revised IRR of RA No. 9184
in all government procurement of the Municipality;
c. OIC–Municipal Accountant to comply with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 on the
proper documentation of disbursements of government funds to establish the
legality, validity and propriety of the transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor convene the SWM Board (i) hasten
the preparation of the 10-year SWM Plan for 2017-2025; (ii) see to it that the 10-year
SWM Plan is approved by the Palawan Council for Sustainable Development pursuant
to R.A. No. 7611; (iii) ensure the establishment of MRF in every barangay, in
accordance with Section 32 of R.A. No. 9003 and its implementing rules and
regulations; and (iv) hire additional personnel under the Office of MENR to assist the
MENR Officer to ensure that its functions are properly carried out.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the Solid Waste
Management Board (i) cause the immediate closure of the open dumpsite; (ii) identify
location for sanitary landfill free from encumbrances to develop and operate as a final
disposal site for solid wastes which shall be designed and operated in accordance with
the guidelines/criteria mentioned under RA 9003; and (iii) strictly comply with RA
9003, otherwise known as the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant cause the immediate
recording of the receivable relative to audit disallowance under ND Nos. 16-001
to 005-101-(15)on the basis of the corresponding NFDs dated October 30, 2017 in
accordance with Section 60 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and Section 22.6
of the RRSA.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to locate the supporting papers and
other reports pertaining to the unremitted BIR withholding taxes amounting to
₱4,151,170.03for CY 2016 and prior years and cause its immediate remittance to the
BIR. Henceforth, ensure strict compliance to Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 dated
April 17, 1998.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as head of the GAD Focal
Point System to:
a) cause the timely preparation, submission, review and approval of GAD Plan
and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report by the proper authorities;
b) update the GAD Code, if necessary, in coordination with the Sanggunian and
disseminate the same to the public in accordance with the guidelines provided
under PCW Memorandum Circular No. 2009-1 dated September 16, 2009;
c) establish GAD sex-aggregated data or GAD database to serve as basis in
performance-based gender responsive planning; and
d) adopt the HGDG tool to ensure that all Municipal employees, including
planning and finance officers, are effectively capacitated on GAD processes.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive cause the immediate
submission of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for CY 2017
AAR and henceforth, observe strict compliance to COA Memorandum No. 2014-
002 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended that management initiate the investigation of the identified discrepancy amounting to ₱604,016.38 and reconcile/adjust the cash in bank account with the Bank Reconciliation Statement to assure accuracy of Cash account as reported in the financial statements. Assign additional manpower/personnel to handle the preparation of BRS and submit the same to the Auditor within 10 days after the end of each month in compliance with COA Circular 96-011.
observations.managementAction:
The OIC - Municipal Treasurer reviewed its cash books, checks issued and bank statements as well as other reports to determine/locate the discrepancies. The OIC-Mun. Treasurer and OIC-Mun. Accountant reconciled the identified discrepancies and the OIC-Mun. Accountant recorded and adjusted the necessary account and draw Journal Entry Voucher for the adjustment. Necessary supporting documents were attached to the JEV.
Additional man-power was requested to the Municipal Mayor thru the HRMO.
observations.reason: Additional man-power for accounting office is still not possible due to the lack of budget for the additional plantilla position as well as absence of qualified applicants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to trace/locate the related documents for unidentified properties to substantiate the reported PPE balances of ₱36,982,865.69 and thereafter maintain the corresponding PPE ledger cards. The Municipal Mayor should likewise prioritize the creation of the Inventory Committee headed by the Municipal GSO to conduct the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU, to prepare and reconcile the RPCPPE with the accounting records, and to furnish the Audit Team with the RPCPPE. During the actual count, the type, description, serial number, acquisition date, name/designation of employee accountable, location and condition of the property counted shall be indicated in the Inventory Sheet. Serially pre-numbered inventory tags shall also be pasted to the counted item/property for easy identification. Further, the Municipal GSO should maintain the required Property Cards per category of PPE. Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs) should also be issued for all newly acquired PPE including those counted during physical inventory.
observations.managementAction: Requested the Municipal Mayor for the additional Man-power, thru the HRMO.
observations.reason: Lack of man-power to assist the tracing/locating of the documents. Inventory committee is yet to be established to handle the inventory of the serviceable and non-serviceable PPEs.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended that: the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to submit the required transaction documents and reports on collections and disbursements every month to the OIC - Municipal Accountant and to strictly observe the policy guidelines on collections and disbursement of government funds such as follows: All payments must be made by checks as prescribed under the principle of imprest system. But when certain conditions required in COA Circular No. 97-002 are met, cash payments may be allowed provided it shall be made by a duly designated Disbursing Officer through his cash advance. Bank withdrawal for municipal expenses is properly monitored to avoid using funds from collections. Claims must be supported with duly approved Payroll/Disbursement Vouchers (DV), Obligation Request (ObR) duly signed by the Municipal Budget Officer and other required documents prior to the release of payment. Collections shall be deposited intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day. Employ cost-cutting measures particularly on local travels which are not urgent, hiring of pump boat or shuttle van, granting of financial assistance or donations in kind, procurement of fuel, office supplies and food supplies/meals/snacks, and hiring of consultants and contractual/job order employees charged to various accounts of Repairs & Maintenance and Other Maintenance & Operating Expenses; devise and implement revenue-enhancing strategies such as aggressive collections of local taxes to help compensate or offset the deficit; recover from the former Municipal Treasurer, Larry I. Eugenio the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents corresponding to his cash accountability of ₱34,835,676.00 and collect any unused cash thereof; and ensure that the 20% Development Fund, DRRMF and Trust Fund shall be utilized exclusively in fulfillment of their respective purposes. The Municipal Budget Officer to maintain the required RAAOs and verify the balances of appropriations in the RAAOs before certifying as to the existence of available appropriation in the Obligation Requests (ObR). Likewise, he should prepare and submit to the Auditor and the Sangguniang Bayan the Status of Appropriations, Allotments, Appropriations and Balances (SAAOB) not later than the twentieth day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction:
Personnel concerned underwent adequate training to familiarize them to handle the work to be done in various offices concerned. Hired qualified municipal personnel, requested the Mayor, thru HRMO, especially for the position of Municipal Accountant.
Initiated among department heads to coordinate with each other especially on financial reports and prompt submission of documents and reports.
The Municipal Budget Officer to maintain the required RAAOs and verify the balances of appropriations in the RAAOs before certifying as to the existence of available appropriation in the Obligation Requests (ObR). Likewise, he should prepare and submit to the Auditor and the Sangguniang Bayan the Status of Appropriations, Allotments, Appropriations and Balances (SAAOB) not later than the twentieth day after the end of each month.
observations.reason:
Failure to properly maintain various reports and documents especially in the MBO to monitor the amount of appropriation, allotment and obligation for a particular budget period.
The distance of the island municipality to the AGDB creates a tendency to use/borrow fund from collections to defray municipal expenses.
Lack of adequate/qualified municipal personnel to properly execute/carry-out the job.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC - Municipal Budget Officer and OIC – Municipal Accountant strictly observe Item No. 2 of COA Circular No. 2005-001 dated Feb. 28, 2005 and Section 4, paragraph (h) Volume I of COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002 and henceforth maintain the required registries of appropriations/allotments and obligations in order to avoid juggling of funds and internal control purposes and to aid the preparation of SCBAA.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed various COA circulars, rules and regulations to familiarize with the forms and reports being maintained. Coordinated with the accounting office to facilitate the preparation of SCBAA.
observations.reason: Lack of qualified and number of personnel for the Municipal Budget Office..

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the OIC - Municipal Accountant to prepare and update the general and subsidiary ledgers to substantiate the balances in the financial statements in accordance with Section 111 and 114 of PD 1445 and Section 70 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Hired a qualified municipal accountant to facilitate the maintenance of necessary general and subsidiary ledgers.
observations.reason: The newly hired Municipal Accountant is needing more time to catch-up with the backlogs at the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC – Municipal Accountant to (i) limit the use of account “Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses” (05-02-99-990) for expenses not otherwise classified under the specific maintenance and other operating expenses accounts; (ii) review charges to account “05-02-99-990” and subsequently prepare adjusting entries by debiting appropriate expense or asset account and crediting account “05-02-99-990”; and (iii) consistently compare estimated to actual expenses in the preparation of Annual Budget based on the accurate classification of financial transactions as documented and supported by accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Hire qualified personnel in the accounting office. Instructed the Accounting Office and its personnel to be more prudent in using the proper account.
observations.reason: Lack of qualified personnel in the accounting office and wide knowledge/background in government accounting. Bulk of papers being processed all at the same time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to exert extra effort in finding qualified applicants and appoint a permanent Municipal Accountant that commensurate the qualifications mandated under Article Four, Section 474 of RA No. 7160, to prepare and update the financial reports of the municipal government for the benefit of the end users.
observations.managementAction: Conducted intensive recruitment/advertisement for the vacant position of Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Unable to find suitable applicant for the position.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Officials headed by the Local Chief Executive of the Municipality implement proper and strong internal control system and appoint/designate competent personnel in order to achieve proper record keeping, completeness of the supporting documents in order to avoid possible loss or misuse of government funds in accordance with the guidelines, policies and procedures of Sections 47 to 49 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM), Volume III and Section 124 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Enhanced the knowledge of concerned department head and its personnel on various laws, rules and regulations by attending seminars, workshops and conferences.
observations.reason: Oversight and laxity in the observance of rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: Strictly adhere with the provisions set forth under COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 to properly control the utilization of fuel; Strictly comply with the provisions 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (R-IRR) of Republic Act No. 9184 in all government procurement; Strictly comply with the Section 4 of PD No. 1445, and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on the documentary requirements in all government transactions; Submit to the Auditor the relevant basic documentary requirements lacking in the disbursement vouchers such as Summary of Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels and Driver’s Trip Tickets otherwise, said transactions shall be suspended in audit; Submit to the Auditor a complete and updated list of serviceable municipal vehicles to ascertain the reliability of fuel consumption and for validation purposes.
observations.managementAction: Requested the hiring of additional man-power in the Accounting Office and various offices/departments concerned.
observations.reason: Lack of qualified man-power to facilitate the maintenance of reports and forms. Weak internal control to check the completeness of supporting documents before processing for payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: OIC - Municipal Accountant to (i) exercise extra diligence in the performance of his pre-audit functions; (ii) be vigilant in the review of the required documents needed for specific transactions; and (iii) be extra cautious in certifying the completeness and propriety of supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers. The Municipal Health Officer or the Requisitioning Officers for the procurement of drugs and medicines be required to certify as to the conformity of procured medicines with the essential drug list as contained in the Philippine National Drug Formulary, to ensure delivery of effective medicines to the general public. Members of the Bids and Award Committee (BAC), Technical Working Group and BAC Secretariat (i) submit the basic documentary requirements found lacking in the reviewed contracts to facilitate the conduct of technical review and evaluation of the mentioned contracts, more particularly those pertaining to infrastructure projects; and (ii) observe strictly the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 to prevent issuance of audit suspension.
observations.managementAction: Retrieval of additional documentary requirements for the purchase of land is ongoing. Familiarized with the rules and regulations pertaining to the purchase of land.
observations.reason: Lack of sufficient/competent and qualified personnel in the accounting office to provide instruction and require the necessary supporting documents for each transaction. Internal control was not properly executed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with Section 4 of PD 1445 and Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the documentary requirements in the purchase of real properties and submit to the auditor the required documents to support the payment. Likewise, strictly comply with Section 444 (b) (1) (vi) of RA No. 7160 on the authority of the Municipal Mayor in entering obligations on behalf of the Municipality; and Section 88 (1) of PD 1445 on the advance payment.
observations.managementAction: Retrieval of additional documentary requirements for the purchase of land is ongoing. Familiarized with the rules and regulations pertaining to the purchase of land.
observations.reason: Purchase was made in haste to secure immediately the procured land. For that reason, additional documentary requirements were not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of Annex “H” Item V (C) of Republic Act No. 9184 in the alternative mode of procurement under the shopping method. Furthermore, ensure that BAC strictly complies with Republic Act No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations in the entire agency’s procurement.
observations.managementAction: Attended training and seminars about RA No. 9184 to familiarize them with the processes and procedures of government procurement.
observations.reason: Disregard for rules and regulations and RA No 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: BAC and OIC-Budget Officer to prepare the PPMP and APP and make sure that these PPMPs and APPs were carefully planned and scheduled to better manage the procurement, produce economical result, make the procurement procedures more efficient and practical. Ensure that the APP adheres with guidelines prescribed by Appendix “2” of R-IRR of RA 9184; BAC should exert extra effort in the planning preparation of the procurement to attract number of bidders to join the bidding to promote competitive bidding; Municipal Mayor to instruct the BAC to create a TWG to assist them in the procurement process. Likewise, ensure that the BAC secretariat to properly carry out its functions and responsibilities in accordance to Section 14.1 of RA 9184; BAC to strictly comply to Section 10 of RA 9184 and all the requirements and procedures in the R-IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 to adhere to the principles of transparency, competitiveness, accountability, equity, efficiency, and economy in its procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Attended training and seminars on RA 9184 to familiarize them with the processes and procedures of government procurement.
observations.reason: Utter disregard for rules and regulations on procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: OIC – Municipal Accountant and OIC – Municipal Treasurer to (i) strictly comply with the timely rendition of accounts and financial reports so as not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (ii) observe the deadline of submission to the Audit Team the required financial reports and documents; OIC – Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit the required Status of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) for CY 2017; Municipal Mayor to (i) require all accountable officers concerned to submit all the financial documents and for each month for the preparation of the financial reports and (ii) impose sanctions against the accountable officers who continuously failed to abide with the timely submission of financial documents and reports as stipulated in Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: OIC – Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit the required Status of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) for CY 2017; Municipal Mayor to (i) require all accountable officers concerned to submit all the financial documents and for each month for the preparation of the financial reports and (ii) impose sanctions against the accountable officers who continuously failed to abide with the timely submission of financial documents and reports as stipulated in Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code of the Philippines.
observations.reason: Lack of man-power and qualified accounting personnel and Municipal Accountant to properly execute the function of the accounting office and timely preparation and submission of financial reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (i) instruct the BAC and the Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion; (ii) ensure that the Local Development Council, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time; (iii) realign or reprogram the unutilized/unimplemented PPAs identified to Priority Development PPAs in accordance with DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; and (iv) conduct intensive review of the continuing projects funded under the 20% DF to determine if the PPAs are still part of the vital needs of the Municipality otherwise reprogram the identified PPAs to priority PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Recruited replacement for the MPDC position to carry out the functions of the office. Fast-tracked the unimplemented projects and determined which of those requires realignment or reprogramming subject to the approval of the sanguniang bayan.
observations.reason: The former MPDC was not able to function well due to illness and that there was a long delay on recruiting qualified applicant for the position. Municipal Engineer was focused on the current year’s projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should strictly observe the posting of public notice for all the projects in conspicuous places in the site projects indicating sufficient and relevant information about the projects in order to promote transparency and accountability and encourage public participation and to secure the right of the people to know information in compliance to COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-101 dated September 23, 2010.
observations.managementAction: Signboards were prepared for all projects of the municipality. All details required by the COA circulars and DILG were also considered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to transfer SET collections for CY 2017 to the SEF and deposit the same to the depository account maintained by the Municipality in compliance with Section 272 of RA 7160 and Sections 82 and 86 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. Likewise, the OIC-Municipal Accountant should strictly observe the separate recording of RPT and SET transactions.
observations.managementAction: Continuous monitoring was done to properly transfer 1% RPT accruing to SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as chairman of the Local School Board, to (i) instruct the Local School Board to convene, prepare and submit Annual Local School Board Budget to ensure that the expenses to be charged in SEF with proper appropriation; (ii) ensure that the functions of LSB are properly undertaken in accordance to Section 99 of RA 7160; and (iii) strictly observe the DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 dated January 19, 2017 on the preparation of SEF Budget and its utilization.
observations.managementAction: LSB Budget was already submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management to provide at least 1% of Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the LCPC and prepare the LDPC and LIPC to ensure the full strengthening and implementation of the LCPC. Strictly comply to Section II items 1(b) and 1(c) of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Proposed budget already included appropriation of 1% LCPC in accordance to DILG Memo. Circular No. 2012-120

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the SWM Board to (i) hasten the preparation of the 10-year SWM Plan for 2017-2025; (ii) see to it that the 10-year SWM Plan is approved by the Palawan Council for Sustainable Development pursuant to R.A. No. 7611; (iii) ensure the establishment of MRF in every barangay, in accordance with R.A. No. 9003 and its implementing rules and regulations; and (iv) hire additional personnel under the Office of MENR to assist the MENR Officer to ensure that the function of that office are properly carried out.
observations.managementAction: SWM Plan was already submitted to the Audit Team. MRF establishment are still on-going in other barangays. MENRO already coordinated all Punong Barangays for the establishment of MRF.
observations.reason: MRF establishment are still on-going in other barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct that Municipal Environmental and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) and the Solid Waste Management Board to (i) stop the use of open dumpsites and immediately close the dumpsite, (ii) identify possible location for sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes. The landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned under RA 9003 and (iii) strictly comply with RA 9003, otherwise known as the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.
observations.managementAction: Identification of the suitable location of the proposed sanitary landfill is still on-going. Coordinated with the DENR to assist in the search/identification of possible site location.
observations.reason: There were encumbrances in the identified land purportedly for the location of the sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the assigned LDRRMO to furnish the Audit Team with the required Annual LDRRMFIP and the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF for CY 2017, as required under Section 5.1.2, 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of the COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2002 and Section 12 (6) & (7) of RA No. 10121 otherwise known as the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction Management Act of 2010. Henceforth, strictly observe the deadlines on the submission of those reports in accordance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Furthermore, ensure that the LDRRMFIP is based on the approved National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan (NDRRMP) 2011-2028, validated and accustomed to the local needs encompassing disaster risk reduction and management such as disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response, and disaster rehabilitation and recovery.
observations.managementAction: Investment plan for the DRRM were attached in the Annual Budget.
observations.reason: Delay on the preparation of the supporting documents for the report on utilizations of DRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Person and the members of the Technical Working Group: (a) cause the preparation of the GAD database in coordination with the LPDO which will serve as basis for planning, implementing, and formulating GAD responsive activities; (b) use monitoring tools such as the HGDG and PIMME in monitoring and evaluating GAD activities; and (c) submit to the Audit Team the annual accomplishment report for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Attended seminars, workshops and conferences with regard to GAD planning and budgeting. Revisited various COA Circular, laws, rules and regulations to familiarize with GAD implementation.
observations.reason: Lack of coordination among offices with regard to the information needed in the preparation of GAD database. HGDG was employed as a basis in identifying GAD responsive PPAs in the municipality; however, lacked additional knowledge on its proper use.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Municipal Mayor and other concerned Officials and Employees to immediately stop the practice of reimbursing actual expenses already covered by the per diem claimed during official travel. Expenses related to the reception and entertainment of LGU visitors/guests shall only be to the extent of the representation allowances authorized by law pursuant to Section 343 of RA 7160. Disbursements found to be irregular, unnecessary and excessive shall be disallowed in audit.

The Municipality should likewise comply strictly with the procedure provided in RA No. 9184 in the procurement of food supplies. Payments should also be made through the issuance of checks to suppliers/creditors as prescribed under the principle of Imprest System provided in COA Circular No. 97-002.

We further recommended for the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure the existence of available appropriations prior to signing Obligation Requests (ObR) for food supplies expenses.

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should review the completeness of supporting documents attached before affixing his signature in the disbursement vouchers. He should likewise be cautious in recording the disbursements to appropriate account to present correctly the Food Supplies account in the financial statements, contrary to Section 305 of RA 7160. Moreover, travel claims were not supported with the requited documents such as travel order and certificate of appearance, thus, the validity and propriety of the transaction could not be ascertained. Travel allowances or per diems were also not in accordance with the prescribed rates provided in Executive Order No. 298.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor that the Travel Order to be issued to the municipal officials and employees should indicate the duration of travel.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant examine the proper classification of accounts as to the nature of transactions in order to payment of claims for traveling expenses of all municipal officials and employees. Claims should also be supported with original copies of the required documents. Likewise, they should ensure that all claims for traveling expenses shall be within the authorized per diems or rates of travel allowance prescribed in Executive Order No. 298. Excessive claims for travel allowance shall be disallowed in audit.
